note: Hand-curated executive headliners whose housing-portfolio appointments or rulemaking actions shape national policy. Region-specific housing ministers are sourced separately from data/politicians/{country}.json.

politicians.name: Donald Trump
politicians.summary: Trump's housing agenda in the second term runs through Scott Turner at HUD, confirmed by the Senate on 5 February 2025. Signals so far have emphasized regulatory streamlining and Opportunity Zones rather than expanded federal supply programs.
keyPoints: Nominated Scott Turner as HUD Secretary in late 2024. Senate confirmation landed on 5 February 2025.
Federal housing programs tracked in the US federal housing file. See data/legislation/federal-us-housing.json for the current bill roster.

politicians.name: Xi Jinping
politicians.role: General Secretary, Chinese Communist Party; President of China
politicians.summary: Xi's government handles housing policy through the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development (MOHURD) under Minister Ni Hong. The 2025 State Council Housing Rental Regulations set new safety and structural integrity rules for landlords and rental properties nationwide.
keyPoints: MOHURD, led by Minister Ni Hong, oversees national housing rental policy and the affordable-rental expansion agenda.
State Council passed Housing Rental Regulations on 7 August 2025, requiring rented properties to meet mandatory standards and barring unauthorized changes to load-bearing structures.
